description_long: This object consists of a cluster of four mushrooms with large, rounded caps that are a vibrant orange-red color, each cap adorned with numerous small white spots. The caps are broad and slightly domed, with the largest mushroom centrally positioned and the others arranged around it, varying in size. The stems are thick, cylindrical, and brown, tapering slightly toward the base, which is bulbous and white. The undersides of the caps are not fully visible, but the overall form suggests a classic toadstool appearance, reminiscent of Amanita muscaria. The surface of the caps appears matte with a slightly rough texture due to the white spots. The mushrooms are depicted as if growing from a flat, nondescript surface, with no visible soil or grass.
description: A cluster of four orange-red mushrooms with white-spotted caps and thick brown stems, resembling classic toadstools. The caps are broad and domed, with bulbous white bases.
description_view_0: Three large mushrooms and one smaller one are visible, with the largest cap dominating the center and the smallest mushroom in the foreground.
description_view_1: The largest cap is prominent, with the smallest mushroom now positioned to the right, showing more of the side profiles of the stems.
description_view_2: The cluster is seen more frontally, with all four mushrooms clearly visible and the caps overlapping slightly.
description_view_3: The view highlights the side of the largest mushroom and the arrangement of the smaller mushrooms around it, with the bulbous bases more visible.
